operating_state: Net sales reached $43.2 billion in Q2 2024, increasing 0.6% driven by the SRS acquisition contributing $1.3 billion and new store openings, offset by comparable sales declining 3.3%. Comparable customer transactions decreased 2.2% and average ticket fell 1.3% reflecting macroeconomic uncertainties and persistent high interest rates pressuring home improvement demand. Online sales represented 14.9% of net sales and grew 3.9% year-over-year.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin expanded to 33.4% from 33.0% driven by lower transportation costs and reduced shrink, partially offset by SRS margin inclusion. SG&A increased to 16.5% of sales from 16.1% primarily due to deleverage from negative comparable sales. Depreciation and amortization rose to 1.7% from 1.5% reflecting SRS-related intangible asset amortization and ongoing business investments.
material_events: Completed $17.7 billion acquisition of SRS Distribution on June 18, 2024, establishing the company as a leading specialty trade distributor serving professional roofers, landscapers and pool contractors. Raised $10.0 billion in long-term debt and utilized commercial paper borrowings to fund the transaction. Increased quarterly dividend 7.7% to $2.25 per share in February 2024 and paused share repurchases in March 2024 in anticipation of the SRS acquisition.
macro_exposures: High interest rate environment continues pressuring home improvement demand, contributing to decreased comparable customer transactions and average ticket. Stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted net sales during the quarter. Macroeconomic uncertainties persist, affecting customer spending patterns across merchandising departments.
forward_outlook: Management plans capital expenditures of $3.0 billion to $3.5 billion for fiscal 2024, focused on new stores and customer experience improvements including technology investments. Share repurchases remain paused until excess cash reduces outstanding debt from the SRS acquisition. The company maintains disciplined capital allocation prioritizing business investment, dividend payments, then excess cash return to shareholders.
uncertainty_profile: Business combination accounting for SRS remains subject to measurement period adjustments up to one year from acquisition date, with preliminary fair value estimates requiring significant management judgment. Commercial paper program expanded to $9.5 billion supported by back-up credit facilities, with outstanding balances at quarter-end.
